title: [PATCH v2] net: reject foo packets before NULL skb access
body:
Problem: the foo receive path can dereference skb after foo_parse_header() returns NULL.

How reached: a malformed packet with a zero-length foo header reaches foo_rx() before the existing length check runs.

Impact: a local reproducer can trigger an oops and drop the interface under load.

Change: reject the packet before dereferencing skb. This is correct because no later path needs skb after foo_parse_header() fails.

Verification:
- make allmodconfig
- scripts/checkpatch.pl --strict
- make C=1 drivers/net/foo/
- kselftest net/foo_rx.sh
- runtime reproduced before the patch and no longer reproduces after the patch

Fixes: 123456789abc ("net: add foo packet receive path")
Link: https://lore.kernel.org/r/foo-null-skb-report
Cc: stable@vger.kernel.org
Reviewed-by: Net Reviewer <reviewer@example.org>
Signed-off-by: Human Submitter <human@example.org>
